en.extract:
Abdim's stork (Ciconia abdimii), also known as the white-bellied stork, is a stork belonging to the family Ciconiidae. It is the smallest species of stork, feeds mostly on insects, and is found widely in open habitats in Sub-Saharan Africa and in Yemen. The common name commemorates the Ottoman Albanian Governor of Wadi Halfa in Sudan, Bey El-Arnaut Abdim (1780–1827).


Distribution and habitat
Abdim's stork is found widely in open habitats throughout Sub-Saharan Africa, from the Sahel to South Africa, being absent mainly from forests, dense woodlands and deserts. A smaller populations occurs in Yemen. It breeds colonially in trees, on cliffs or rooftops in the northern half of its range (north of the Equator) during the wet season from May to August, migrating to eastern and southern Africa for the remainder of the year. This stork has escaped or been deliberately released into Florida, U.S., but there is no evidence that the population is breeding and may only persist due to continuing releases or escapes.
Widespread and common throughout its large range, Abdim's stork is evaluated as Least Concern on the IUCN Red List of Threatened Species.  It is the subject of several nationally coordinated breeding programs: in the United States, the plan for this species is administered by the Association of Zoos and Aquariums and in Europe by the European Association of Zoos and Aquaria.


Description
Ciconia abdimii is a black stork with grey legs, red knees and feet, grey bill and white underparts. It has red facial skin in front of the eye and blue skin near the bill in breeding season. It is the smallest species of stork, at 73 cm (29 in) and a weight of just over 1 kg (2.2 lb).


Biology
Abdim's stork is mostly insectivorous, feeding on locusts, caterpillars and other large insects, although these birds will also eat small reptiles, amphibians, mice, crabs and eggs. The female  lays two to three eggs and is slightly smaller than the male.


Gallery


References


External links

Abdim's Stork Ciconia abdimii Archived 2007-09-26 at the Wayback Machine - BirdLife International
Abdim's Stork - The Atlas of Southern African Birds
Abdim's Stork Archived 2016-10-10 at the Wayback Machine - SeaWorld Parks and Entertainment

'''Abdim's stork''' ('''''Ciconia abdimii'''''), also known as the '''white-bellied stork''', is a stork belonging to the family [[Ciconiidae]]. It is the smallest species of stork, feeds mostly on insects, and is found widely in open habitats in [[Sub-Saharan Africa]] and in [[Yemen]].<ref name=IUCN/> The common name commemorates the [[Ottoman Albania|Ottoman Albanian]] Governor of [[Wadi Halfa]] in [[Sudan]], [[Bey El-Arnaut Abdim]] (1780–1827).<ref name ="LPZAbdim'sStork" />

==Distribution and habitat==
Abdim's stork is found widely in open habitats throughout Sub-Saharan Africa, from the [[Sahel]] to South Africa, being absent mainly from forests, dense woodlands and deserts. A smaller populations occurs in Yemen.<ref name=IUCN/> It breeds [[Bird colony|colonially]] in trees, on cliffs or rooftops in the northern half of its range (north of the [[Equator]]) during the wet season from May to August, [[Bird migration|migrating]] to eastern and southern Africa for the remainder of the year.<ref name=IUCN/> This stork has escaped or been deliberately released into [[Florida]], U.S., but there is no evidence that the population is breeding and may only persist due to continuing releases or escapes.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nonnatives - Abdim's Stork |url=http://myfwc.com/wildlifehabitats/nonnatives/birds/abdims-stork |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181213194636/http://myfwc.com/wildlifehabitats/nonnatives/birds/abdims-stork |archive-date=2018-12-13 |website=Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission}}</ref>

Widespread and common throughout its large range, Abdim's stork is evaluated as [[Least Concern]] on the [[IUCN Red List of Threatened Species]].<ref name=IUCN/>  It is the subject of several nationally coordinated breeding programs: in the United States, the plan for this species is administered by the [[Association of Zoos and Aquariums]] and in Europe by the [[European Association of Zoos and Aquaria]].

==Description==
''Ciconia abdimii'' is a black [[stork]] with grey legs, red knees and feet, grey [[Beak|bill]] and white underparts. It has red facial skin in front of the eye and blue skin near the bill in breeding season. It is the smallest species of stork, at {{Convert|73|cm|in|abbr=on}} and a weight of just over {{Convert|1|kg|lb|abbr=on}}.<ref name=seaworld>{{cite web
 |url=http://seaworld.org/AnimalInfo/Animal-Info/Animal-Bytes/Birds/Abdims-Stork 
 |title=Abdim's Stork Animal Bytes 
 |website=SeaWorld 
 |access-date=December 27, 2013 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131227213038/http://seaworld.org/AnimalInfo/Animal-Info/Animal-Bytes/Birds/Abdims-Stork 
 |archive-date=December 27, 2013 
}}</ref>

==Biology==
Abdim's stork is mostly [[insectivorous]], feeding on [[locust]]s, [[caterpillar]]s and other large insects, although these birds will also eat small reptiles, amphibians, mice, crabs and eggs.<ref name=IUCN/><ref name ="LPZAbdim'sStork">{{Cite web |url=http://www.lpzoo.org/animals/factsheet/abdims-stork |title="Abdim's Stork Fact Sheet, Lincoln Park Zoo" |access-date=2011-07-08 |archive-date=2012-08-15 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120815220324/http://www.lpzoo.org/animals/factsheet/abdims-stork }}</ref> The female  lays two to three eggs and is slightly smaller than the male.

zh.extract:
白腹鸛（學名：Ciconia abdimii），是原產非洲和西南阿拉伯半島的一種小型鸛類。棲較乾旱的地帶。食昆蟲，尤喜蝗蟲。


外形
它是鸛屬中最小的鹳，成体长约73厘米，重量仅1千克。
